calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Approving Corrected Trust Amendment Nunc Pro Tunc
Off Calendar Sep-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Shearer for Brian Matza (Petitioner). Daniel Conrad for Noreen Matza. Brian McCaffrey for Michael Matza, Mary Matza, Patrick Matza and Brian Matza.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not Brian has standing, whether or not the petition is proceduraly proper/improper and whether the Court should grant/deny the petition. The Petition for Order Approving Corrected Trust Amendment Nunc Pro Tunc was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Court Reporter: Ken Brill, CSR#12797, (415)274-9977. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy McConoco. (Reported)

Petition For Order Approving Corrected Trust Amendment Nunc Pro Tunc
Off Calendar Jul-29-2014 Continued To Sep-08-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Corrected Trust Amendment Nunc Pro Tunc
Off Calendar Jul-28-2014 Continued To Aug-25-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Terminate, Rescind, Void And/Or Set Aside Amended And Restated Trust Or Trust Provisions: For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Feb-15-2011 Off Calendar

Ex Parte Application As Authorized By The Court, And Supporting Declaration Of Richard M. Shearer For Ex Parte Order Approving Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-21-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Approve Trust Amendment
Off Calendar Jan-04-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Noreen Matza (Trustee/Petitioner). Brian McCaffrey for Michael Matza, Mary Matza, Kevin Matza and Patrick Matza. Richard Shearer for Brian Matza.

The Court read the language in the settlement agreement which states that the Parties agree and are obligated to cooperate in petitioning to the Court for an order approving the agreement. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust was continued to February 15, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the Petition to Approve Trust Amendment (signing the order for the same in open court). The Parties agreed to filing of an ex parte petition to approve the settlement agreement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Terminate, Rescind, Void And/Or Set Aside Amended And Restated Trust Or Trust Provisions: For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Jan-04-2011 Continued To Feb-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Terminate, Rescind, Void And/Or Set Aside Amended And Restated Trust Or Trust Provisions: For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-10-2010 Continued To Jan-04-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Terminate, Rescind, Void And/Or Set Aside Amended And Restated Trust Or Trust Provisions: For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Oct-06-2010 Continued To Nov-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Michael Matza and Mary Matza. Brian McCaffery specially appearing for Daniel Conrad for Noreen Matza. Donna Quan for R. Brian Matza.

Mr. McCaffrey stated that the matter had settled with Judge James at mediation but that the settlement is going to require a petition to amend the trust. The Court continued the matter to November 10, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the filing of the petition to amend the trust. Mr. McCaffrey stated the Mr. Conrad will be the one filing the petition to amend the trust.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.

Petition To Terminate, Rescind, Void And/Or Set Aside Amended And Restated Trust Or Trust Provisions: For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Jul-21-2010 Continued To Oct-06-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Noreen Matza and specially appeariing for Counsel Brian McCaffrey for Mivhael Matza and Mary Matza.

The Court noted that Mr. Bassi was not present. Mr. Conrad requested a continuance stating that all Counsel agreed to mediation, and that mediation was scheduled for September 30, 2010 and October 1, 2010. The matter was continued to October 6, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition To Terminate, Rescind, Void And/Or Set Aside Amended And Restated Trust Or Trust Provisions: For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Jun-23-2010 Continued To Jul-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Bassi for R. Brian Matza. Daniel Conrad for Noreen Matza. Brian McCaffrey for Michael Matza, Mary Matza, and Kevin Matza.

Mr. Bassi stated that he had submitted a proposed order regarding the discovery. Mr. Conrad stated that he and Mr. Bassi sorted out the Court's order after they received the transcript. Mr. Bassi stated that the records were important as to the mediation, that he came into this case late, that there was a settlement reached, that it then broke down with Mr. Matza's previous attorney, and that he thought the parties should try and attend mediation within 45 days since they were close to settlement previously. Mr. Conrad stated that Mr. Matza is suing his 90 year old Mother, that an expedited mediaton isn't possible for the reasons set forth on the record, that they have spent all their time responding to Mr. Bassi's papers, that they need to do their own discovery, and that he believes the Court will grant preference because his client is 90 years old. The Court inquired about mediation. Mr. Conrad responded stating that this isn't a nice case where a man is suing his 90 year old Mother. Mr. Bassi stated that because documents in this case will be important that he is not stipulating to an early trial date. Discussions regarding discovery needed before mediation were held. The Court ordered mediation to be held withing 60 days and continued the matter 30 days for status noting that it would extend the deadline if additional time is needed. Mr. Conrad stated that he did not wish to be at a discovery disadvantage and that he thought 90 days would be likelier to get it done; then the petitioner isn't prejudiced. The Court signed the protective order and order for mediation. Mediation is to be completed by August 25, 2010 and a status hearing on mediation is set for July 21, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
Off Calendar May-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Noreen Matza. Michael Bassi for Brian Matza.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to defect of service of the subpeonas, Attorney/Client priveledge, privacy, the production of records, confidentiality of medical records and the issuance of a protective order. The Court suggested Counsel confer to determine what may by in the medical records and agree to which records may need a protective order. The Court ruled as follows: 1)The Motion to Quash is denied. 2)The records from Ms. O'Keeffe are limited to those of Mr. Matza's consultations and not to the those in which the Doctor consulted Ms. Matza. 3)The Motion to Quash with regard to the medical records is denied. 4)With regard to the protective order, the records should not to be disclosed to any individuals unrelated to this case with out prejudice to filing of a later motion to change the protective order if needed. The Court suggested Counsel contact each other and let one another know what they are going to file. Mr. Conrad is to prepare the order and present it to Mr. Bassi.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Petition To Terminate, Rescind, Void And/Or Set Aside Amended And Restated Trust Or Trust Provisions: For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar May-12-2010 Continued To Jun-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
